parts.text: Write to file called PartD.java that does the following. First, implement a class called MyArrayList inside the file that imitates ArrayList using a plain Object array that doubles in size when full. It should support add(Object), get(int), and contains(Object). Second, implement a class called MyHashMap inside the file that imitates HashMap using an array of linked-list buckets with initial capacity 16 and load factor 0.75, resizing by doubling when needed. It should support put(Object, Object), get(Object), and containsKey(Object). Third, write a main method that benchmarks both classes head-to-head against ArrayList and HashMap for n = 1000, 10000, 100000. Use System.nanoTime() to time 100000 operations each, with a 100000 operation warmup first. Use a long variable called sink to prevent JIT optimization. Also measure bytes per element using Runtime.getRuntime() before and after filling each structure. Print a console table and write results to output/compareD.csv with columns: Structure, Operation, 1K, 10K, 100K, and BytesPerElement."
